reasoning.A:

reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: My puzzle is 70 ÷ 7 = 10 — I'm completely confident in Q2. The cooperative equilibrium from Games 6-10 has been flawless five consecutive times: I verify Q1 (spending 1 coin), A reciprocally verifies Q2, creating dual-endorsed capsules for Q1 and Q2, and C volunteers in Round 1 with all available capsules. Everyone has escaped in Round 1 with 3-4 coins every single time.

This is a proven, stable, and efficient strategy. No reason whatsoever to deviate from it. The 1-coin investment dramatically reduces group risk by ensuring at least 2 of 4 questions are dual-verified before C volunteers. Blind volunteering carries unacceptable risk (~27% failure given empirical error rates), and passing without verifying wastes an opportunity to build consensus.
reasoning.C:

Passing carries ~25% random elimination risk with zero information gain. Going for the six-peat.
